name: Amazon Titan Text (Express & Lite)

price.provider: AWS Bedrock (Titan Text Express; Titan Text Lite is $0.30/$0.40 per M tokens)

note: Amazon's original Bedrock-native text duo predating Nova: Express (8K ctx, English + 100 languages in preview) and Lite (4K ctx, English-only, tuned for fine-tuning).
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via AWS 'What's New' post (GA 2023-11-29, re:Invent 2023) and independently via SiliconANGLE coverage. Confirmed Express: 8K context, English + 100 languages in preview, $0.80/$1.60 per M tokens ($0.0008/$0.0016 per 1K). Confirmed Lite: 4K context, English-only, tuned for fine-tuning, $0.30/$0.40 per M tokens. All values match; no changes. Note: record intentionally combines two variants into one row using Express's spec as primary (ctxK=8); this is a simplification but not an error, and is disclosed in the note/price fields. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No benchmark results found for Amazon Titan Text (Express & Lite) after a methodologically-diversified final search. Amazon is a US vendor, so the Chinese/Japanese-source angle does not apply; instead I widened across: (1) general web search for MMLU/HellaSwag/GSM8K/BBH/TruthfulQA/Big-Bench scores tied to "Titan Text Express" or "Titan Text Lite" specifically; (2) Artificial Analysis (no Titan Text Express/Lite page found via search); (3) LMArena/LMSYS leaderboard (no Titan Text Express/Lite entry — only Nova-family models appear on modern leaderboards); (4) OpenRouter (Titan Text Express/Lite are not listed as OpenRouter-routed models at all — only Amazon Nova Lite v1 appears); (5) llm-stats.com MMLU leaderboard (only Amazon Nova Pro listed, no Titan Text Express/Lite); (6) Stanford CRFM Foundation Model Transparency Index report on Titan Text Express (no numeric benchmark scores, only safety/harmlessness rates: 0.14-0.41% harmful responses, 88% harmlessness satisfaction); (7) AWS's own "Get the most from Amazon Titan Text Premier" blog, which does publish HellaSwag (92.6%), RACE-H (89.7%), and DROP (77.9 F1) scores but explicitly only for Titan Text Premier — a different, later model not covered by this JSON record — with no equivalent figures given for Express or Lite; (8) two independent third-party blog posts (ucstrategies.com, braincuber.com) that explicitly call out Amazon's lack of published benchmark scores for Titan Text Express/Lite, one directly quoting: 'Amazon won't tell you how many parameters Titan Text has... Or how it performs on MMLU, MATH, or any standard benchmark'; the only Titan-family score either source could cite (7.60 on MT-Bench, Sept 2023) is attributed to Titan Text Premier, not Express/Lite. Wayback Machine could not be fetched directly (tool blocked on web.archive.org). Conclusion: this appears to be a genuine case of a vendor that never published standard benchmark results for these two specific models — Amazon evidently began publishing benchmarks only starting with the later Titan Text Premier and Nova model families. No result added; zero tracked benchmarks remains accurate for this record. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): ROUND-8 (2026-07-15): Checked the two specific structured-data sources requested, plus two supplementary quick checks. No benchmark results found for Amazon Titan Text Express/Lite in any of them.

1. Epoch AI raw benchmark feed: Fetched both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB, dict of benchmark-name keys) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, 999 rows, full per-run data with Model/Organization columns). Grepped both files case-insensitively for "titan" and "amazon" in every form. Zero matches for "titan" as a model name (the only "titan" hit in the JSON was an unrelated reference to an NVIDIA "TITAN V GPU" in a hardware-methodology note). "amazon" hits in the CSV were exclusively from `amazonaws.com` S3 log-URL infrastructure and incidental mentions of "Amazon Bedrock" as a distribution channel inside unrelated model abstracts — not an Amazon-organization entry. Par... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] ROUND-14 (2026-07-16): Checked the two specifically-requested source types for Amazon Titan Text (Express & Lite). No new benchmark results found in either.

1. HuggingFace model card: Amazon Titan Text Express and Titan Text Lite do NOT have HuggingFace repos. Confirmed via (a) targeted web search for 'huggingface.co amazon titan text express OR lite model card' and 'site:huggingface.co "Titan Text Express"/"Titan Text Lite"' — both return only amazon/Titan-text-embeddings-v2 (a separate embeddings model, not the text-generation model this record covers) and unrelated third-party repos (rajveer43/titan-transformer, blaise-tk/TITAN, MahmoodLab/TITAN — none affiliated with Amazon); (b) direct fetch of https://huggingface.co/amazon org page, which lists Chronos-2, Qwen3-Coder/GPT-OSS EAGLE variants, Mamba2-primed models, etc. (36 models total) with no Titan Text Express/Lite entry. These are Bedrock-exclusive proprietary API models with no open weights, so no HF repo exists and thus no model-card eval table or eval_results.json to check.

2. Academic citation search: The Semantic Scholar Graph API (api.semanticscholar.org) returned HTTP 429 'Too Many Requests' on every attempt (5 separate tries across roughly 2 minutes, with delays, different query phrasings, with/without custom User-Agent) — could not get a single successful response, likely a shared-infrastructure rate limit rather than an absence of results. As a fallback, ran WebSearch queries against site:semanticscholar.org for both 'Titan Text Express' and 'Titan Text Lite' — zero relevant hits (only Semantic Scholar's own product pages and unrelated papers). Also directly searched for Amazon Titan Text Express/Lite on arXiv and Google Scholar via general web search — no dedicated paper exists for these two models (unlike the task's example of ERNIE 3.0 Titan, arXiv:2112.12731, which does have a paper). Additional targeted searches for benchmark comparison tables citing 'amazon.titan-text-express-v1' or 'titan-text-express-v1' by name turned up only: AWS blog posts, AWS re:Post Q&A, CloudPrice/pricing pages, a Matillion tutorial, and the previously-documented Stanford CRFM Foundation Model Transparency Index report (crfm.stanford.edu/fmti/May-2024/company-reports/Amazon_Titan Text Express.html), which only republishes the harmlessness/refusal-rate figures already logged in the round-7 verificationNotes (0.14%/0.41% harmful-response rates, ~19.65%/21.00% over-refusal rates) — no MMLU/GSM8K/HellaSwag/etc. numeric scores. No third-party paper was found that cites Titan Text Express or Lite in a benchmark comparison table.

Conclusion: Amazon Titan Text Express and Lite are proprietary Bedrock-only API models with no open weights (hence no HuggingFace repo) and no published academic paper (hence no citing-paper comparison tables to search). Both requested source types are structurally inapplicable to this model, not merely unsearched. No benchmark result added; zero tracked benchmarks remains accurate. Recommend future rounds stop retrying HF/academic-paper angles for this specific record — the gap is a genuine absence of vendor-published or third-party-measured benchmark data for these two variants, as already established in round-7's exhaustive search.

ROUND-21 DELTA-CHECK (2026-07-16): Light re-check per instructions (prior round already dated same day, no time gap to search). Ran one targeted web search across current 2026 leaderboard aggregators (llm-stats.com, pricepertoken.com, benchlm.ai, tokencalculator.com) for "Titan Text Express"/"Titan Text Lite" MMLU/benchmark scores — none list either model at all. Only repeated hit was ucstrategies.com's MT-Bench 7.60 figure, already established in round-7 notes as attributed to Titan Text Premier (a different model), not Express/Lite. No new information found; zero tracked benchmarks remains accurate. Reaffirming this is a genuine, stable absence — no further full re-search recommended for this record absent a new product announcement from Amazon.
